data.goal: Audit recovered artifacts and live data provenance, predeclare the strongest defensible real-data flagship, and execute it with strong baselines

data.message: Adopt the manifest-bound CRSP-v2 point-in-time industry-neutral momentum campaign; treat the 2018-2019 legacy run as historical-only because its stated holdout was used for selection and its recovered CRSP provenance is incomplete.
data.rationale: The live 2005-2025 CRSP-v2, stocknames, delists, DSI, and Ken French authorities support a stronger untouched 2023-2025 final holdout, while the legacy RC p-value is 0.941 and ticket 35 explicitly optimized holdout Sharpe.

data.message: Live source audit matched protocol SHA256 b51d50ccfcf1cc368558d49afc5bd9386120efe92bdd03a7d43d7791be971743: 21 CRSP-v2 partitions, 40,402,817 rows, stocknames_v2, stkdelists, DSI, and three Ken French digests; holdout_outcomes_read=false. Native test-fast passed 135 tests and restricted-data policy scanned 1,075 files successfully.

data.message: CRSP-v2 adapter repair at 07acd25d truthfully distinguishes permitted header/full-byte side-table scans from materialized rows, excludes post-validation rows in selection receipts, enforces the required 0.15 industry gross cap, and stages no-clobber output publication. Live source audit matched protocol f88df6c9 across 21 partitions/40,402,817 rows; 10 focused tests, 139 full native tests, touched-file ruff, run-log validation, and restricted-data policy passed. No full panel or empirical result was run.

data.summary: Predeclared CRSP-v2 flagship adapter is repaired, independently accepted, source-audited, strictly verified, reconciled into the canonical SSD repo, and published on scoped/recovery branches. Empirical execution is intentionally moved to a dedicated memory-heavy lifecycle; no full panel or performance result has yet been run.

data.goal: Build and validate the full real-data CRSP-v2 selection panel through 2022, execute the frozen six-candidate flagship selection with strong baselines, and freeze the selected model without opening final-holdout outcomes

data.message: Run the sole memory-heavy microalpha selection build at code tree 579e65f4 and protocol f88df6c9 with DuckDB memory_limit=4GB, SSD-local temp spill, and no-clobber run root 20260710T232307Z_selection. Audit all 21 manifested 2005-2025 partition headers, but open outcome rows only for the 18 partitions through 2022; do not open 2023-2025 daily outcomes or materialize post-validation side rows.
data.rationale: This follows the corrected frozen access contract, fits the 36GB host with a bounded 4GB DuckDB allocation and 749GB free SSD spill capacity, and preserves the final holdout.

data.message: Bounded CRSP-v2 overlap reconciliation opened only the 18 permitted daily partitions through 2022: 27 PERMNOs, 92,027 affected daily rows, zero rows without a date-valid stocknames record, 21,837 overlapping daily rows, and zero nulls in every daily point-in-time universe/industry field (ticker alone had 612 nulls and is not an eligibility field). Attribute disagreement is real—especially SIC, conditional type, and exchange—so stocknames cannot safely override the direct CIZ daily row.
data.ref: CRSP CIZ/SIZ cross-reference plus live 2005-2022 aggregate diagnostic

data.message: For the CRSP-v2 panel, use each CIZ daily row as the authoritative point-in-time source for PERMCO, SIC, universe filters, exchange/status, and ticker. Treat stocknames_v2 as a date-coverage audit only: require at least one date-valid record, report overlaps, and never overwrite direct daily attributes from an ambiguous name-history view.
data.rationale: Official CIZ documentation places point-in-time security/issuer fields directly on the time-series row; the live bounded diagnostic found complete daily eligibility fields but conflicting overlapping stocknames states. This semantics correction is frozen before any candidate performance is observed.

data.message: Retire the artificial 4GB DuckDB cap after two distinct execution methods both reached the same 3.7GiB internal allocation ceiling during rolling-feature materialization. Run the committed same-filesystem spill implementation once at a bounded 12GB DuckDB limit with two threads; keep microalpha as the portfolio's sole memory-heavy lane and keep all restricted WRDS bytes local.
data.rationale: Live headroom is approximately 18.4GiB, so a 12GB engine cap leaves material operating-system headroom. The prior failures were resource-bound after the source-semantics correction, not logic failures; AWS transfer remains unnecessary and unlicensed.
data.ref: live memory_pressure -Q: 51% free of 36GiB; commits 6830c61f and f366e5da

data.message: The committed 12GB/two-thread selection build completed in 86.45s with 8.82GB peak RSS and zero swap. Artifact 4ed2b33e2496e224a7701c3d0d71d593909d8fc7547ecdcbc483b2c83686206a contains 1,587,343 monthly rows, 18,013 PERMNOs, 338,428 eligible rows, and 8,508 delisting pseudo-days through 2022. All 18 permitted daily partitions were opened; no 2023-2025 outcome partition or post-validation side-table row was materialized.

Freeze the CRSP-v2 selection implementation at commit 27a05381 before observing any candidate return: signal windows are formation lags 1..11 and 1..5 (realization-relative t-12..t-2 and t-6..t-2), all six candidates share the same t+1 mapping, constrained execution LP, costs, eligibility gates, and lexicographic reducer. The historical 40-name row is included as hash-bound noncomparable evidence.
data.rationale: The runner passed 150 native tests, restricted-data policy, focused chronology/delist/capacity/reducer/baseline/tamper/no-clobber tests, and real baseline coverage checks before candidate metrics were viewed.

data.message: Correct the frozen selection execution-availability rule before any candidate metrics exist: a requested position with no following-month observed trading session receives zero executable delta, while an already-held security with no row is carried at an unchanged mark with last observed point-in-time metadata until a session or delisting appears. Any present realized row without a CIZ return remains a hard stop. Replay all six candidates and baselines from scratch at commit 8692b90a.
data.rationale: The stopped pre-metric run found two requested names (0.6419% absolute target weight) with no January 2017 panel/raw row, price, ADV, flag, or delisting record. There was no next session on which the order could execute, so treating them as filled positions with missing returns was incorrect. Focused chronology, constrained-capacity, absent-session, delisting, and return tests pass.

data.message: Bounded identifier-free diagnostic at code 8692b90a and panel 4ed2b33e classified the replay hard stop: one carried validation-period position in March 2017 maps to 23 CIZ NT/status-X placeholder rows, with zero executable-price, numeric-return, or delisting rows. No identifiers, raw rows, candidate metrics, or post-2022 outcome partitions were emitted or opened.

Predeclare the repaired replay semantics before any candidate metric: next-month trade availability still requires a positive observed price; a held name with a wholly absent month or a present CIZ placeholder month containing no price, numeric return, or delisting is carried unchanged with prior point-in-time metadata and cannot receive a trade; a numeric return remains the authoritative mark even if no price is available; any present row with an executable price or delisting but no CIZ return remains a hard stop. Replay the unchanged frozen six-candidate grid and reducer from scratch.
data.rationale: The bound diagnostic found only non-trading NT/status-X placeholders at the failure edge: 23 source rows, zero executable-price, return, or delisting rows. The correction distinguishes session evidence from derived monthly-row existence without using candidate outcomes or weakening the executable missing-return stop.

data.message: The predeclared placeholder correction is committed locally at d1221ee7/tree 7da2e4ee and published through the GitHub connector at remote commit 9745b5b1 with the identical tree 7da2e4ee on branch portfolio-os/microalpha-selection-execution-20260710. Exact checks passed: 21/21 targeted CRSP-v2 tests, 150/150 fast tests, restricted-data policy, run-log validation, local-source import, focused Ruff, and diff checks.

The frozen six-candidate validation replay completed in 41.71s at 1.163GB peak RSS with zero swap. All six candidates satisfied structural eligibility; the predeclared lexicographic reducer froze blend_12_2_6_2__inverse_vol_126d. Independent review verified all 10 artifact hashes, 72 validation months, exact reducer order, panel/protocol bindings, exposure/cap invariants, zero restricted identifier columns, and zero final-holdout outcome access. Validation evidence is modest rather than promotional: net HAC Sharpe 0.2407 (t=0.8398), CAGR 1.70%, max drawdown 11.42%; it trails the identical-universe classic by 0.0853 Sharpe and CRSP value-weighted market by 0.3904, and the harshest predeclared cost stress is -0.0915 Sharpe. No alpha or promotion claim is supported before the sealed holdout.

MICROALPHA-CANONICAL-VERIFY-001: the validation selection and frozen model are complete and independently audited, but two-phase Project OS finish is withheld because the trusted registry validates the dirty canonical checkout rather than exact sibling-worktree tree 7da2e4ee/artifact 09f7d524. The canonical checkout contains unrelated post-pause uncommitted panel/test edits that must be preserved; validating or overwriting it would not prove this result. Reconcile the exact published tree without losing those bytes or add an artifact/worktree-bound verifier, then verify and finish.
data.rationale: The configured verifier and registry root do not bind the executed sibling worktree or external result artifact, while the canonical checkout has overlapping uncommitted work from the prior manager. Project OS evidence must match the exact code tree and result generation.

data.message: MICROALPHA-CANONICAL-VERIFY-001 resolved: unrelated canonical bytes are preserved on codex/microalpha-canonical-wip-preserve-20260711 at 44b67acc, and canonical execution authority now derives from exact selected-worktree tree 7da2e4ee with the shared journal retained.
data.rationale: The unrelated canonical WIP is preserved at 44b67acc; the canonical checkout now derives from exact executed tree 7da2e4ee with the complete shared event journal restored, so native verification can bind the result code generation without overwriting prior bytes.
